text.title: CD Pipelines and Deployment Strategies Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the cd-pipelines methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Pipeline has discrete jobs: build, unit, integration, staging, e2e, production. Each job is independently retriable.
rule.rationale: Combined stages hide failures and prevent partial retries; isolation enables targeted rollback.

rule.statement: Production deploy uses an explicit strategy: rolling / blue-green / canary. Strategy is configured per service.
rule.rationale: Implicit 'just deploy' produces drop-everything outages; explicit strategy bounds blast radius.

rule.statement: Each deploy step waits on /healthz returning 200 with the new version before proceeding to next step.
rule.rationale: Skipping health checks shifts traffic onto a still-warming process; user errors spike.

rule.statement: Pipeline auto-rolls back on guardrail breach (error rate or latency >2× baseline for 5 min).
rule.rationale: Humans are slower than the alarm; auto-rollback shrinks MTTR by minutes.

rule.statement: Every production deploy emits a deployment_frequency event with version + sha + duration.
rule.rationale: DORA visibility is the feedback loop that improves CD adoption.

rule.statement: Canary deploys ramp 5 → 25 → 50 → 100% with health check pause between steps.
rule.rationale: Small initial cohort exposes regressions before majority traffic shifts.

rule.statement: If cd-basics prereqs are not met, or workload is batch run-to-completion, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Concrete pipeline YAML needs the prereqs solved first.
